stem: What is the sum, in degrees, of the interior angles of a convex polygon with \( [[n]] \) sides?
solution: A convex polygon with \( n \) sides can be split into \( n-2 \) triangles, each contributing \( 180^{\circ} \). So the interior-angle sum is \( (n-2)\cdot 180 = ([[n]]-2)\cdot 180 = [[answer]] \) degrees.
distractor_rationales: This forgets to subtract 2; it uses \( n \) triangles instead of \( n-2 \).
This subtracts only 1 instead of 2 from the number of sides.
This uses \( 90^{\circ} \) per triangle instead of \( 180^{\circ} \).
This is the sum of the exterior angles (always \( 360^{\circ} \)), not the interior angles.
This uses \( 360^{\circ} \) per triangle instead of \( 180^{\circ} \).
This is off from the correct value by ninety degrees.

stem: A convex polygon with \( [[n]] \) sides has one exterior angle marked at each vertex (formed by extending each side). What is the sum, in degrees, of all \( [[n]] \) exterior angles?
solution: For any convex polygon, walking once fully around the outside turns through a full \( 360^{\circ} \) rotation regardless of the number of sides. So the exterior angles always sum to \( [[answer]]^{\circ} \), independent of \( n=[[n]] \).
distractor_rationales: This is the interior-angle sum formula, confused with the exterior-angle sum.
This assumes each exterior angle is a full straight angle.
This is half of the correct total.
This is one interior angle of a regular \( n \)-gon, not the exterior sum.
This multiplies the correct total by \( n \) unnecessarily.
This doubles the correct total.

stem: What is the measure, in degrees, of one interior angle of a regular polygon with \( [[n]] \) sides?
solution: The full interior-angle sum \( (n-2)\cdot 180 \) is split evenly among the \( n \) equal angles of a regular polygon, so one angle measures \( \dfrac{(n-2)\cdot 180}{n} = \dfrac{([[n]]-2)\cdot 180}{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \) degrees.
distractor_rationales: This is the total interior-angle sum, not a single angle.
This is the measure of one exterior angle, not one interior angle.
This forgets to subtract 2 from \( n \) before dividing.
This uses \( 180/n \) instead of the correct interior-angle formula.
This is off from the correct value by one degree.
This is off from the correct value by one degree.

stem: What is the measure, in degrees, of one exterior angle of a regular polygon with \( [[n]] \) sides?
solution: The exterior angles of a regular \( n \)-gon are equal and sum to \( 360^{\circ} \), so one exterior angle measures \( \dfrac{360}{n} = \dfrac{360}{[[n]]} = [[answer]] \) degrees.
distractor_rationales: This is one interior angle, not one exterior angle.
This multiplies instead of dividing by \( n \).
This uses half the exterior-angle sum by mistake.
This is the total interior-angle sum, not a single exterior angle.
This is off from the correct value by one degree.
This is off from the correct value by one degree.

stem: The sum of the interior angles of a convex polygon is \( [[S]]^{\circ} \). How many sides does the polygon have?
solution: Solving \( (x-2)\cdot 180 = [[S]] \) for \( x \) gives \( x = \dfrac{[[S]]}{180} + 2 = [[answer]] \) sides.
distractor_rationales: This divides the sum by 180 but forgets to add back the 2.
This adds 1 instead of 2 after dividing by 180.
This subtracts 1 instead of adding 2 after dividing by 180.
This divides by 360 (the exterior-angle sum) instead of 180.
This is off from the correct number of sides by one.
This is off from the correct number of sides by one.

stem: Each interior angle of a regular polygon measures \( [[angle]]^{\circ} \). How many sides does the polygon have?
solution: Each interior angle of a regular \( x \)-gon is \( 180 - \dfrac{360}{x} \). Setting this equal to \( [[angle]] \) and solving gives \( x = \dfrac{360}{180-[[angle]]} = [[answer]] \) sides.
distractor_rationales: This treats the angle as a simple fraction of 180 rather than solving the interior-angle equation.
This uses 180 instead of 360 in the numerator of the correct formula.
This treats the given interior angle as if it were the exterior angle.
This inverts the correct formula.
This is off from the correct number of sides by one.
This is off from the correct number of sides by one.

stem: Each exterior angle of a regular polygon measures \( [[ext]]^{\circ} \). How many sides does the polygon have?
solution: Each exterior angle of a regular \( x \)-gon is \( \dfrac{360}{x} \). Setting this equal to \( [[ext]] \) and solving gives \( x = \dfrac{360}{[[ext]]} = [[answer]] \) sides.
distractor_rationales: This uses 180 instead of 360 in the numerator.
This inverts the correct formula.
This multiplies instead of dividing.
This confuses the exterior-angle formula with an interior-angle-style expression.
This is off from the correct number of sides by one.
This is off from the correct number of sides by one.

stem: The measures of the interior angles of a convex polygon with \( [[n]] \) sides form an arithmetic sequence: each angle, taken in order, is \( [[d]] \) degrees larger than the previous one. What is the measure, in degrees, of the largest interior angle?
solution: The average interior angle is \( \dfrac{([[n]]-2)\cdot 180}{[[n]]} \), and for an \( n \)-term arithmetic sequence this average equals the average of the first and last terms. So the largest angle exceeds the average by \( \dfrac{(n-1)d}{2} \), giving \( \dfrac{([[n]]-2)\cdot 180}{[[n]]} + \dfrac{([[n]]-1)\cdot [[d]]}{2} = [[answer]] \) degrees.
distractor_rationales: This is just the average interior angle, without adjusting for the sequence's spread.
This subtracts the spread instead of adding it, giving the smallest angle instead of the largest.
This adds \( n\cdot d \) directly to the full angle sum instead of adjusting the average correctly.
This uses \( n \) instead of \( \frac{n-1}{2} \) as the multiplier on \( d \).
This is off from the correct value by the common difference.
This is off from the correct value by the common difference.
